name: RMP-Regulated Pressurized Chemical Configuration
description:
The Green Valley Water Facility (Colchester, VT) recently installed a new industrial refrigeration system using Anhydrous Ammonia. As the Environmental Engineer, you must add this chemical to the facility's Tier II submission. 

1. Import the baseline facility data from C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Tier2Tasks\green_valley_baseline.t2s into the already open EPA Tier2 Submit 2025 application.
2. Add a new chemical to the inventory: Anhydrous Ammonia (CAS: 7664-41-7).
3. Mark it as a Pure chemical, an EHS (Extremely Hazardous Substance), and subject to CAA Section 112(r) RMP provisions.
4. Select the following physical/health hazards: 'Gas Under Pressure', 'Flammable (gases, aerosols, liquids, or solids)', 'Acute Toxicity (any route of exposure)', and 'Skin Corrosion or Irritation'.
5. For the inventory amounts, set the Maximum Daily Amount to 18500 lbs and the Average Daily Amount to 7200 lbs, with 365 days on site.
6. Add a storage location with the description 'Building C - Mechanical Room, Main Refrigeration System'. Set its Pressure to 'Above ambient pressure' and Temperature to 'Below ambient temperature'.
7. Save the chemical and use File > Create a Submission File to export the updated facility submission exactly to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Tier2Output\rmp_chemical_added.t2s.
